title: DSDS Design Specifications Document Block
description: The visual specs of a component: property values, spacing, size limits, typography, and how it responds to breakpoints. Makes every visual decision explicit and measurable, so design and code stay in sync.

designValue.description: A design value. If the system has tokens, this MUST be a token identifier (e.g., 'color-action-primary', 'space-4'), not a raw value — that keeps it from drifting out of sync with the token system. A raw CSS value (e.g., '#0055b3', '16px') is only allowed when there's no token layer.

designProperties.description: A map of property name to value. Keys say what the value controls (e.g., 'background', 'min-height') and MUST be lowercase kebab-case. Values are token names ('color-action-primary') or raw CSS ('#0055b3', '16px') — use either, or mix them.

spacingSpec.description: Spacing around and inside the component. `internal` is spacing the component owns; `external` is spacing it recommends but the parent layout controls.

internal.description: Padding and gaps inside the component. Keys name the relationship (e.g., 'icon-to-label') and MUST be lowercase kebab-case. Values are token names or raw CSS (e.g., 'space-4', '8px').

external.description: Recommended margins between this component and its siblings or container. Keys name the relationship (e.g., 'button-to-button') and MUST be lowercase kebab-case. This is a recommendation — the parent layout has final control.

sizingSpec.description: The component's size limits — min/max width and height, and any aspect ratio.

minWidth.description: Minimum width as a token name or raw value (e.g., 'size-button-min-width', '64px').

maxWidth.description: Maximum width as a token name or raw value (e.g., 'size-dialog-max-width', '600px', 'none').

minHeight.description: Minimum height as a token name or raw value (e.g., 'size-button-min-height', '44px').

maxHeight.description: Maximum height as a token name or raw value.

aspectRatio.description: Aspect ratio as a ratio string (e.g., '16/9', '1/1', '4/3').

typographyEntrySpec.description: Typography for one text element in the component. Maps CSS typography properties to token names or raw values. If `typeToken` is set, the individual properties just document what it resolves to.

fontFamily.description: Font family as a token name or raw value (e.g., 'font-family-body', 'Inter, sans-serif').

fontSize.description: Font size as a token name or raw value (e.g., 'font-size-body', '14px', '0.875rem').

fontWeight.description: Font weight as a token name or raw value (e.g., 'font-weight-regular', '400', 'bold').

lineHeight.description: Line height as a token name or raw value (e.g., 'line-height-body', '1.5', '20px').

letterSpacing.description: Letter spacing as a token name or raw value (e.g., 'letter-spacing-body', '0.01em').

typeToken.description: A single token covering multiple typography properties at once (e.g., '$body-compact-01', 'type-scale-body-sm'). When set, the individual properties just describe what it resolves to.

typographySpec.description: Typography for each text element in the component. Keys name the element (e.g., 'label', 'helper-text') and MUST be lowercase kebab-case.

responsiveEntry.description: How the component adapts at one breakpoint.

breakpoint.description: The breakpoint name or value at which this behavior applies (e.g., 'small', 'medium', 'large', '768px', 'min-width: 1024px').

description.description: How the component's layout, sizing, or behavior changes at this breakpoint.

designSpecifications.description: The component's default, baseline specs — properties, spacing, size, typography, and responsive behavior. Per-variant and per-state overrides don't go here; they live on the `variants` and `states` blocks.

kind.description: Identifies this block as a design specifications spec.

responsive.description: How the component adapts across breakpoints, smallest to largest.
